title: VLA Leaderboard Entries
description: data/leaderboard.json. Written by refine.py, sync_external.py, and manual edits. Rendered by leaderboard/site/app.js. validate.py enforces this schema plus benchmark-aware rules (score range, aggregation, official-leaderboard policy, citation coverage).

last_updated.description: ISO date of the most recent data change. Bump when results changes.

results.description: One entry per (model × benchmark × reported_paper). Sorted by (benchmark, model).

model.description: BibTeX-style key. First-party: {method_bib} (e.g. 'kim24openvla'). Third-party: {method_bib}__{measuring_paper_bib}. The measuring paper is the one that ran or first reported the number.

display_name.description: Site-rendered method name. First-party: 'OpenVLA'. Third-party: 'OpenVLA (from X-VLA)', where the parenthesized paper matches the measuring paper.

name_in_paper.description: Method label verbatim from the source paper's results table. null for manual or API-synced entries with no paper label.

overall_score.description: Aggregate that controls ranking. Set only when the entry uses the benchmark's standard protocol (all Checks pass). Otherwise null, with the paper's raw aggregate stored in task_scores.reported_avg.

suite_scores.description: Sub-scores over declared suites. Keys match benchmarks.json when overall_score is non-null.

task_scores.description: Per-task scores. Key 'reported_avg' is reserved for a paper-reported aggregate that could not be ranked.

reported_paper.description: URL of the paper that measured this number. null for API-synced entries.

notes.description: Caveats (non-standard subset, training budget, architecture class, non-arxiv cite source).

model_paper.description: URL of the paper that introduces the method. Distinct from reported_paper.

weight_type.description: shared = same checkpoint across benchmarks. finetuned = trained on this benchmark's data.
